title: Accounts Config (v1)
description: GitHub account mappings for directory-based auto-switching. Lives at ~/.claude/local/accounts.config.json (never committed). Supports one personal account and zero-or-more work accounts. The SessionStart hook reads this to decide which GitHub identity to activate based on the current working directory matching a dir_trigger entry.

personal.description: The personal GitHub account. Exactly one is required — use this account for personal repos and open-source contributions.

user.description: GitHub username. Must match GitHub's username rules: 1-39 chars, alphanumeric or single hyphens, cannot start or end with a hyphen.

dir_triggers.description: Directory path prefixes (one or more) that activate this account when the working directory falls under any of them. Supports leading ~ for home-relative paths.

work.description: Zero-or-more work GitHub accounts. Each entry is an independent identity with its own directory triggers. Work accounts default to public_blocked: true — new repos cannot be made public without explicit override.

user.description: GitHub username for this work account. Same rules as personal.user.

dir_triggers.description: Directory path prefixes that activate this work account.

public_blocked.description: When true (recommended default for work accounts), the harness blocks operations that would make a repo public (gh repo create without --private, gh repo edit --visibility public). Omit or set false only for work accounts explicitly authorized to publish open-source projects.
